body.title: [sprint] Rename ~/.unseen_university/flags → run_flags (self-documenting breaker dir); keep mechanism
body.intention: I intend that runtime dirs say what they do, so a future reader doesn't mistake the live auto-run breaker dir for dead cruft and delete it.
body.description:
**Problem:** `~/.unseen_university/flags` was flagged as an 'old folder' to remove, but it is the LIVE circuit-breaker directory: ground_loop reads `_FLAGS_DIR / '<name>.breaker'` to suppress a device's auto-start (plugin_proxy.py:53,56; plugin_daemon.py:42,49; documented daemon.py:11). Empty-but-present is its normal resting state. The name 'flags' hides its purpose — it gates device auto-RUN.
**Proposed shape:** Rename the dir to `run_flags` (says what it does). Update `_FLAGS_DIR = _IGOR_HOME / 'flags'` (ground_loop/daemon.py:38) → `uu_home() / 'run_flags'`, and every breaker-path builder (plugin_proxy.py:53, plugin_daemon.py:42) + the docstring (daemon.py:11). KEEP the breaker mechanism intact. While touching daemon.py, replace the stale `_IGOR_HOME` alias (a retired-IGOR_HOME-name holdover) with `uu_home()` from `unseen_university._uu_root`. Dir is currently EMPTY → no file migration needed; ensure the code mkdirs `run_flags` before touching a breaker.
**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/ground_loop/daemon.py, plugin_proxy.py, plugin_daemon.py.
**Design rules:** keep the breaker semantics identical; no new ~/.unseen_university path without the rename being the only change.
**Scope boundary:** rename + alias fix only; do NOT change breaker logic or add new flags.
**Completion criteria:** grep `_FLAGS_DIR.*flags'` and `_IGOR_HOME` in ground_loop → 0 stale refs; breaker path resolves to `~/.unseen_university/run_flags/<name>.breaker`; ground_loop imports green; touching a `.breaker` still suppresses auto-start.
**Test plan:** unit: breaker_path resolves under run_flags and `is_tripped` reads it; ground_loop import-green; grep-gate on the old `flags` path + `_IGOR_HOME`.
